name: DurationDatePicker

unions.name: DatePicker
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular date picker instance.

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: The type facilitating the configuration of the datepicker settings.
aliases.type: { formattedDay : Time.Weekday -> String.String, formattedMonth : Time.Month -> String.String, today : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, dayDisabled : Time.Posix -> Basics.Bool, internalMsg : DurationDatePicker.DatePicker -> msg, selectedMsg : Time.Posix -> Time.Posix -> msg }

values.name: closePicker
values.comment: Close the provided date picker and receive the updated picker instance.

values.name: defaultSettings
values.comment:
A record of default settings for the date picker. Extend this if
you want to further customize the date picker.

Requires a MsgConfig msg record to set the date picker's internal and external
msg indicators

    { internalMsg : DatePicker -> msg
    , externalMsg : Posix -> msg
    }

values.name: init
values.comment: Instantiates and returns a date picker.

values.name: openPicker
values.comment:
Open the provided date picker and receive the updated picker instance. Also
takes a default time the picker should center on (in the event a time has not yet
been picked) as well as the picked start and end times. A common example of a default time
would be the datetime for the current day.
values.type: Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> DurationDatePicker.DatePicker -> DurationDatePicker.DatePicker

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Events external to the picker to which it is subscribed.

values.name: view
values.comment:
The date picker view. Simply pass it the configured settings
and the date picker instance you wish to view.
